pr.title: run permuter on all remaining low-mismatch functions
pr.author: lukechampine

summary: Broad low-mismatch decomp-matching pass generated by sorting remaining functions by mismatch count and running a permuter/Codex workflow. The PR touched 34 files across fighter, game-mode, stage, interface, library, menu, map, player, and vi code. The author explicitly reports reverting many data-layout-related matches, such as inline-string-to-variable swaps, because data layout work should wait until the whole translation unit is ready to link.

key_files.role: Camera matching edits in Camera_8002CB0C, including variable introduction/renaming and chained zero initialization.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: Camera_8002CB0C selected_slot and zoom_dir/x_val/y_val initialization changes

key_files.role: Fighter common matching edit extracting an inline helper for ftCo_800AFC40's x1A88 item/ray update block.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: static inline ftCo_800AFC40_inline0 introduced and called

key_files.role: Fighter bury matching edit adding a small getter inline for fp->mv.co.bury.translate.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_Bury_GetTranslate introduced and used in PSVECAdd

key_files.role: Fighter item throw matching edit using a tightly scoped global_optimizer pragma and a register-allocation-preserving null check/comment.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: #pragma push/global_optimizer off around ftCo_LightThrowDash_Phys with comment 'Needed for matching register allocation.'

key_files.role: Kirby init cleanup/matching edits: many includes removed and several Kirby special-N calls changed from ftCo_8009D704 to specific ftCo_8009Dxxx functions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: include list reduced; ftKb_SpecialN_* call replacements

key_files.role: Fighter action matching edits replacing (1 / 256.0f) expressions with 0.003906f multipliers.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftAction_80071028, ftAction_8007169C, ftAction_80073008 literal multiplier changes

key_files.role: Game-mode matching edit using clearer pointer temporaries while preserving copied destination usage and PAD_STACK.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: temp_r6 renamed/split to dst and copied_dst in fn_8016D634

key_files.role: Game-mode matching edits adding a typed extern struct for lbl_8047368C and a named entry struct for lbl_803D9498 table access.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_1832_8047368C_t with game_type field; gm_80186F6C_Entry table type

key_files.role: Game-mode matching/naming/data-placement edit replacing a local static s32 with an address-commented .sdata2 extern and renaming permuter slop locals to role names.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbl_804DA78C changed from static initializer to .sdata2 extern; new_var* locals renamed

what_changed: Used a script to sort remaining functions by mismatch count, then ran Codex/permuter on a broad batch of low-mismatch functions.
Touched 34 files with 330 added and 286 deleted lines according to the PR metadata.

Kept selected source-level matching edits such as local type/lifetime changes, small helper inlines, literal spelling changes, include reduction, pointer temporary reshaping, and scoped optimizer steering.
Reverted many data-layout-related matches discovered by the workflow, specifically including cases like swapping an inline string for an explicitly-defined variable.

In ftCo_ItemThrow.c, wrapped ftCo_LightThrowDash_Phys in #pragma push / #pragma global_optimizer off / #pragma pop and added a no-op-looking cd != NULL block documented as needed for matching register allocation.
In gm_1832.c, introduced typed structs for data access patterns: gm_1832_8047368C_t for lbl_8047368C.game_type and gm_80186F6C_Entry for a static table.
In gm_18A5.c, renamed permuter-style new_var locals to role names such as created_text, text_color_word, text_color, color_word, and created_text2.

smart_moves.text: The author explicitly filtered the permuter output and reverted data-layout wins that relied on replacing inline literals with explicit variables, keeping the PR focused on code-match progress rather than speculative data ownership.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: 'Many of the new matches it found were data-layout-related... I reverted those; that stuff should wait until the whole TU is ready to be linked'

smart_moves.text: The broad batch was seeded by mismatch count, which is an evidence-backed triage method for finding low-effort matching opportunities.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: 'script to sort all the remaining functions by mismatch count'

smart_moves.text: Where optimizer steering was used, the diff shows a tight push/pop scope around one function rather than a broad file-level pragma.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_ItemThrow.c #pragma push/global_optimizer off before ftCo_LightThrowDash_Phys and #pragma pop after it

smart_moves.text: Some permuter-generated or generic local names were replaced with role names in the final diff, improving reviewability while retaining the match-oriented shape.

decomp_lessons.lesson: Tool-assisted permutation can be useful at scale when candidates are triaged by mismatch count, but its output needs human filtering before review.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: script sorted remaining functions by mismatch count and Codex ran the permuter
human_pr_text: author says the approach seems fruitful

decomp_lessons.lesson: Data-layout wins from a code-matching permuter run, especially replacing inline strings with explicit variables, should be treated as risky and deferred unless the TU/linking context supports them.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: data-layout-related matches like swapping inline string for explicit variable were reverted

decomp_lessons.lesson: Small local type or accessor changes can be part of matching work when they express existing data more directly, as seen with typed table entries and field access in gm_1832.c.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_1832_8047368C_t added with game_type field
raw_diff: gm_80186F6C_Entry type introduced for lbl_803D9498

decomp_lessons.lesson: Pragmas and no-op-looking register allocation nudges can appear in matching PRs, but the evidence here supports treating them as narrow exceptions requiring explicit justification rather than general style.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_ItemThrow.c scoped global_optimizer off and comment 'Needed for matching register allocation'

naming_conventions.convention: Replace temp_rXX/new_var-style names with role names when the local role is clear, while keeping address-style global names when semantics are not proven.
examples: created_text, created_text2, text_color_word, text_color, color_word in gm_18A5.c
gm_80186F6C_Entry for table entries in gm_1832.c
evidence_refs: raw_diff: camera.c valid renamed/split to selected_slot
raw_diff: gm_16AE.c temp_r6 replaced by dst/copied_dst
raw_diff: gm_18A5.c new_var locals renamed to role names
raw_diff: gm_1832.c named table entry typedef

naming_conventions.convention: Address-style symbols remained for data globals where broader semantics were not established, while local struct field names captured only the observed layout role.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Sort unmatched functions by mismatch count, then run a permuter over low-mismatch targets to find easy matches.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: 'script to sort all the remaining functions by mismatch count'
human_pr_text: 'had Codex run my permuter on a bunch of them'
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: Good triage tactic, but the same body text shows generated candidates included data-layout temptations that required reverting.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Avoid accepting data-layout-related permutations, such as inline literal/string replacement with explicit variables, when the PR is focused on code matching and the full TU is not ready for linking.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: This is strongly supported as a curator candidate because it aligns with both PR body and loaded standards.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use tiny static inline helpers to alter codegen or aliasing/lifetime shape around a repeated expression or field access.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: Evidence shows the tactic was used, but the PR slice does not provide objdiff output proving why each helper was necessary.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use exact literal spelling changes for floating constants when it affects codegen, such as replacing (1 / 256.0f) with 0.003906f.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftaction.c multiplier changes in ftAction_80071028, ftAction_8007169C, and ftAction_80073008
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: Diff supports use of the tactic; no validation logs are loaded.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Adjust local signedness, local lifetime, and pointer aliases to influence generated code.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_1601.c val u32 to s32
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: The edits are visible, but exact mismatch improvement is not documented in the loaded evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Apply a tightly scoped #pragma global_optimizer off when a single function requires register allocation steering.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_ItemThrow.c scoped pragma around ftCo_LightThrowDash_Phys
raw_diff: comment 'Needed for matching register allocation.'
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: Loaded standards treat pragmas as exceptions needing proof; PR slice has only an inline comment, not objdiff/regression evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Reduce unnecessary includes as part of source cleanup during matching.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftKb_Init.c include list reduced from many headers to a shorter set
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: Diff supports cleanup, but no require-protos or build evidence is loaded.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Low-mismatch permuter batches need human filtering
accepted_candidate_records.text: A mismatch-count-sorted permuter/Codex batch can produce useful easy wins, but the PR author explicitly filtered the output and reverted data-layout-related matches before merging.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: script sorted remaining functions by mismatch count

accepted_candidate_records.title: Defer data-layout substitutions in code-matching PRs
accepted_candidate_records.text: When a code-matching pass finds data-layout-related wins such as replacing inline strings with explicit variables, defer those changes unless the TU/linking/data ownership evidence is in scope.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: 'swapping an inline string for an explicitly-defined variable' was reverted
human_pr_text: 'that stuff should wait until the whole TU is ready to be linked'

source_update_candidates.title: Add PR 2586 as supporting example for text-before-data matching
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2586 provides historical evidence for deferring data-layout-related permuter wins, specifically inline-string-to-explicit-variable substitutions, until the whole TU is ready to link.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: author reverted data-layout-related matches and said they should wait until the whole TU is ready to link
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR body directly supports the existing standards against chasing data/literal ownership during focused code matching.

source_update_candidates.title: Document low-mismatch sorting as a candidate-discovery tactic, not validation
source_update_candidates.text: Mismatch-count sorting plus permuter/Codex runs can be recorded as a candidate-discovery workflow, but accepted matches still require normal local verification and review filtering.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: script sorted remaining functions by mismatch count and ran the permuter
human_pr_text: author manually reverted a class of generated matches
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR supports usefulness for discovery, while the lack of loaded verifier output means it should not be promoted as validation.

source_update_candidates.title: Use PR 2586 as cautionary evidence for pragmas/register-allocation tactics
source_update_candidates.text: The diff includes a narrowly scoped #pragma global_optimizer off block with a comment that a no-op-looking cd != NULL check was needed for register allocation. Curator may link this as an example of a tactic that requires narrow scope and explicit evidence.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_ItemThrow.c #pragma push/global_optimizer off/#pragma pop around ftCo_LightThrowDash_Phys
raw_diff: comment 'Needed for matching register allocation.'
source_update_candidates.reason: The loaded evidence shows the tactic and local comment but not objdiff/regression proof, so it should be curated as a cautionary example rather than broad permission.

source_update_candidates.title: PR 2586 touched a broad set of low-mismatch files across systems
source_update_candidates.text: The PR changed 34 files across camera, fighter, game-mode, stage, interface, library, menu, map, player, and vi paths as part of a broad low-mismatch permuter pass.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: full list of 34 changed files
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful for future retrieval of broad permuter/matching passes, but should remain a historical path fact candidate.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that every touched function matched; the PR slice lacks objdiff/checkdiff logs and the diff is truncated.
Do not promote #pragma global_optimizer off or no-op register allocation checks as ordinary accepted style; loaded standards define them as exceptions needing evidence.
Do not infer reviewer approval rationale; there are no loaded review comments or reviews.
Do not turn the gm_18A5.c .sdata2 extern change into a global data ownership fact without current symbol/split evidence.
Do not claim the permuter/Codex workflow itself validates matches; the author describes it as fruitful but also reports reverting a class of generated results.
